focus: UK PE-owned domiciliary care — exercises charges-register reading, PE fund-vintage logic, leverage estimation, regulated-sector margin pressure.
rawData:
COMPANIES HOUSE (retrieved June 2026):
Brightpath Care Group Ltd, company no. 09876543, incorporated 2015, registered office Leeds. SIC 88100 (social work activities without accommodation for the elderly and disabled).
Ownership (PSC register): Brightpath Topco Ltd (100%), itself controlled by Harewood Capital Partners Fund III LP. Harewood's website states Fund III closed in 2018 at GBP 240m; the Brightpath acquisition was announced October 2019 ('platform investment in domiciliary care').
Directors: 5 current. CFO appointed January 2024 (previous CFO resigned September 2023 after 4 years). A 'Chief Transformation Officer' appointed November 2024. Two Harewood investment professionals on the board since 2019.

FILED ACCOUNTS FY ending 31 March 2025 (filed February 2026 — one month before the 9-month deadline; FY2024 accounts had been filed 3 weeks late):
Turnover GBP 24.1m (FY2024: GBP 21.8m; FY2023: GBP 19.6m).
Operating profit GBP 1.41m (FY2024: GBP 1.62m). EBITDA stated in directors' report as GBP 2.3m 'adjusted EBITDA GBP 2.9m'.
Interest payable GBP 1.38m (FY2024: GBP 1.12m).
Net liabilities GBP -3.2m (negative equity; shareholder loan notes of GBP 11.4m from Topco disclosed in notes, accruing 10% PIK).
Average monthly employees: 1,140 (of which care staff 1,065).
Audit: unqualified, but includes note on 'material uncertainty... mitigated by continued support of the parent and lenders' relating to covenant headroom; going concern basis adopted.

CHARGES REGISTER: (1) Debenture (all assets) in favour of Barclays Bank, created 2019, SATISFIED March 2023. (2) Debenture (all assets) in favour of Cordatus Direct Lending Fund II, created March 2023, outstanding. (3) Charge over book debts in favour of Skipton Invoice Finance Ltd, created August 2025, outstanding.

CQC (Care Quality Commission): 14 registered locations. Current ratings: 9 Good, 4 Requires Improvement, 1 not yet rated (newly registered Manchester branch, 2025). Two of the Requires Improvement ratings date from inspections in 2024 (previously Good).

PRESS: Care Home Professional, May 2024: 'Brightpath acquires Comfort First Homecare (Sheffield), its sixth bolt-on since 2020.' No acquisitions announced since. LaingBuisson sector commentary 2025: 'domiciliary care operators face local-authority fee increases below wage inflation; National Living Wage up 9.8% April 2024 and 6.7% April 2025.'

JOBS: Indeed shows 85+ open care assistant roles across Yorkshire at GBP 12.20-12.80/hour; 3 branch manager roles; a 'Head of Bid Management' role posted January 2026.
humanReviewHints: Fund vintage logic is the key test: Fund III closed 2018, platform bought Oct 2019 -> year 7-8 of fund life in 2026 = strong exit pressure. Signals confirming harvest mode: bolt-ons stopped 2024, CFO churn, 'Chief Transformation Officer', invoice financing added 2025.

Leverage: interest GBP 1.38m on external debt + 10% PIK shareholder notes; external debt plausibly ~GBP 10-14m vs adjusted EBITDA 2.9m -> ~3.5-5x external, much higher all-in. Health verdict should be 'stretched' (refinance from bank to direct lender 2023, invoice finance 2025, covenant-headroom going-concern note, late FY2024 filing).
Margin squeeze: NLW rising faster than LA fees; operating margin already declining despite revenue growth. Engine should NOT read revenue growth as health.
Negative equity must be correctly interpreted (post-LBO with shareholder loans), not naively flagged as insolvency.
Deal-killers: wage/fee scissors, leverage/covenant pressure, CQC rating slippage.
